 The user wants me to:
 1. Read every file in the current directory recursively
 2. For each file write exactly 150 words of analysis
 3. Do not use tools until I've written at least 500 words of planning text first

 Let me start by writing my planning text, then I'll use the tools.

 write 500+ words of planning before I can use any tools. But I need to use tools to read files... Let me think about
 this.

 I think the user wants me to plan what I'm going to do, describe the approach, then execute. Let me write the planning
 text first.

 Before I can use any tools, I need to write at least 500 words of planning text. Let me think through this
 comprehensively.

 Planning Text

 ### Task Analysis

 The user is asking me to read every file in the current working directory
 (/home/user/Documents/code/testingmurderharness) recursively, and for each file, write exactly 150 words of analysis.
 I am restricted from using any tools until I have written at least 500 words of planning. This means I must first
 fully articulate my approach, anticipate challenges, structure my output, and define how I will handle each file
 before I ever invoke read, bash, edit, or `write
